zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Emma Stafford于1326年出生在英国越可爾葛拉芙頓时，她的父亲Thomas Stafford26岁，母亲Mrs Thomas Stafford27岁。她于1341年与Geoffrey De Dethick在英国英格兰德比夏爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。她去世在英国英格兰德比夏爾。

nameMeanings.name: Stafford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name principally from Stafford (Staffordshire), but occasionally from other places with similar names, such as Stafford House in Ifield (Sussex), possibly East and West Stowford in East Down (Devon), and three minor places in Devon called Stafford, in Dolton, Broadhembury, and Colyton parishes. The places are all named with Old English ford ‘ford’ as the final element, but have different initial elements. The Staffordshire placename has Old English stæth ‘river bank, shore’; Stafford in Colyton (Devon) has Old English stān ‘stone, rock’; the Sussex placename has Old English stēor ‘steer, bullock’; East and West Stowford (Devon) have Old English stæf ‘staff, stave, rod’; Stafford in Dolton and Stafford in Broadhembury (Devon) may have Old English stæth, stān, or stæf.

variants.name: Stocker
meanings: German and Swiss German (also Stöcker): topographic name for someone living by a tree stump (see Stock 3) or an occupational name for a tree cutter, from Middle High German stocken ‘to clear land’.
German and Swiss German (also Stöcker); Dutch: occupational name from Middle High German, Middle Dutch stocker ‘jailer’.

English: occupational name from Middle English stokker ‘one who sells stockfish’ (fish dried in the air without salt). This was the usual source of the name in medieval London, where a bylaw of 1419 stated that no stokker should board a ship to buy fish (presumably in order to forestall the market).

nameMeanings.name: Emma
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, originally a short form of compound names such as Ermintrude containing the word erm(en), irm(en) ‘entire’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, but its popularity in medieval England was greatly enhanced by the fact that it had been borne by the mother of Edward the Confessor, herself a Norman. In modern times, it was only in moderate use early in the 20th century but rose sharply in favour in the 1970s and has since remained perennially popular.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Emma Stafford was born in 1326, in Grafton, Yorkshire, England, her father, Thomas Stafford, was 26 and her mother, Mrs Thomas Stafford, was 27. She married Geoffrey De Dethick in 1341, in Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. She died in Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Emma Stafford est née en 1326, à Grafton, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Stafford, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Mrs Thomas Stafford, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé Geoffrey De Dethick en 1341, à Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Elle est décédée à Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Emma Stafford 1326 in Grafton, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Stafford 26 und ihre Mutter Mrs Thomas Stafford 27. Sie hat 1341 in Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom Geoffrey De Dethick geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Sie starb in Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Emma Stafford è nata il nel 1326 nel Grafton, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, Thomas Stafford, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Mrs Thomas Stafford, aveva 27 anni. Lei sposato Geoffrey De Dethick nel 1341, nel Derbyshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì nel Derbyshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ko.lifeSummary: Emma Stafford(이)가 1326년 영국 요르크실르 그라프턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Stafford(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs Thomas Stafford(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1341년 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르에서 Geoffrey De Dethick(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Emma Stafford родилась в 1326 г. в местности графтон, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Stafford было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs Thomas Stafford, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Geoffrey De Dethick в 1341 г. в местности дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Она умерла в местности дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания.
